steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: Rewrite the right side: $-97 + x^2 = x^2 - 97$. The equation becomes $x^2 - 97 = x^2 - 97$, which is an identity — true for every real $x$.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Zero: An identity has solutions, not zero. ✗
  • Choice B — Exactly one: No single value is singled out; every $x$ works. ✗
  • Choice C — Exactly two: Same trap as B — no restriction limits solutions to two. ✗
  • Choice D — Infinitely many: Every real number satisfies the identity. ✓
That is the correct answer.
